name: Adventure Learning Programs
summary: The mission of ALPs is to challenge people through adventure-based learning to discover themselves and understand those around them. We offer team building workshops operating under the philosophy of Experiential Education.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers - UW Madison Chapter
summary:
ASME promotes student development in the Mechanical Engineering discipline through industry, academic, and community involvement.
Students are not limited to Mechanical Engineering majors.

name: Wisconsin Robotics
summary: A multi-disciplinary student organization that specializes in the design, construction, and programming of competitive teleoperated and autonomous robots.

name: Kesem at University of Wisconsin-Madison
summary: Kesem UW-Madison is a national community, driven by passionate college student leaders, supporting children through and beyond their parent's cancer.

name: Korean Undergraduate Students Association
summary: KUSA is an umbrella organization for Korean undergraduate students on UW-Madison campus. KUSA functions as a "hub" which connects and supports other various student organizations that are run by Korean students.

name: Choi Tae Kwon Do Club
summary: The Choi Tae Kwon Do Club at UW-Madison is a martial arts club started by Drs. Jack Nitschke and Carmon Alonso in 1998. The club is dedicated to promoting individual growth through a non-competitive environment. ALL CLASSES ARE FREE!

name: Supporting Peers in Laidback Listening
summary: SPILL is a peer-to-peer anonymous support network made of and for UW-Madison students! Students can confidentially "spill" using our website, and they'll receive support, resources and encouragement from fellow Badgers who can relate.

name: Chinese Undergraduate Student Association (UW-Madison)
summary: CUSA serves as a supportive student group dedicated to improving the well-being of all undergraduate Chinese and other Asian students at UW-Madison and fostering cultural exchange on campus and Madison community.

name: Young Progressives, The
summary: An progressive organization with a commitment to activism, grassroots politics, and professional development for future political professionals. All majors welcome.

name: SAE Baja Team, UW-Madison
summary: The SAE Baja Team's purpose is to provide students with hands-on experience and apply what they have learned to real-world problem-solving situations.  This is accomplished by designing and building a prototype single-seat, off-road vehicle.

name: Steel Bridge Team of the University of Wisconsin-Madison
summary: The Steel Bridge Team-UW competes in the National Student Steel Bridge Competition each year. The competition challenges engineering students to design, fabricate, and build a 1:10 scale steel bridge that conforms to the competition's rules.

name: Out for Business
summary: Out for Business is a student-run organization for LGBTQ+ students within the Wisconsin School of Business community.

name: Middle Eastern Law Students Association
summary: MELSA fosters communication, companionship, and advancement of students of Middle Eastern descent and those interested in the Middle East. MELSA represents the diverse range of political, economic, legal, and cultural perspectives of the Middle East.

name: AHANA-MAPS Pre-Health Society
summary: AHANA is a student organization grounded in the beliefs of providing students with an essential, well-rounded perspective of the healthcare field, enriching experiences, and community service opportunities.

name: Geological Engineering Student Chapter
summary: GLE Student Chapter is an outlet for students to explore the fields of geological engineering. We host speakers from industry and academia, go on field trips, attend national and regional meetings, and plan social events within the department.

name: American Foundation for Suicide Prevention Wisconsin Chapter - UW Madison
summary:
AFSP-UW

AFSP-UW seeks to eliminate the stigma of mental illness on the University of Wisconsin-Madison campus through educating the student body about suicide and mental health and promoting available resources for professional help.

name: American Nuclear Society, University of Wisconsin Student Section
summary: ANS is comprised of students, primarily nuclear engineers, who have a shared interest in learning about nuclear science and technology and serving our local community.

name: WUD Music
summary: WUD Music is a group of passionate UW-Madison students who work year-round to program and promote the weekly concerts at Memorial Union and Union South. We handle all aspects of event planning from negotiation, to contracting, to day-of-show execution.

name: Environmental Law Society
summary: The Environmental Law Society welcomes all students interested in the application of law to environmental issues at the state, national, and international levels.

name: Greater University Tutoring Service
summary: GUTS is dedicated to empowering students as active, independent learners and cultivating a prosperous educational environment. Students, peer tutors, and staff work together through a system of cooperative learning.

name: Human Powered Vehicle Challenge Team
summary: HPVC provides an opportunity for engineering students to gain valuable hands-on experience as they design, build, and race a vehicle that can be utilized as an alternative to an automobile.

name: Kappa Sigma (Beta Epsilon)
summary: Kappa Sigma is a men's social fraternity which is located at 124 Langdon Street. The Fraternity is committed to fostering a lifelong brotherhood, promoting academic and professional achievement, and providing a unique social experience.

name: Psi Chi Honor Society, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: Psi Chi is the International Honor Society in Psychology, founded in 1929 to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship while advancing the science of psychology. Psi Chi is an affiliate of the American Psychological Association.

name: Risk Management and Insurance Society
summary: An organization focused on providing Risk Management & Insurance students with opportunities to learn from professionals in the industry, meet others students and RMI faculty, and discover the variety of career options in the field.

name: Beta Alpha Psi - Sigma Chapter
summary: The Sigma Chapter of Beta Alpha Psi is an honorary organization for both graduate and undergraduate accounting students with the objective of encouraging and giving recognition to scholastic and professional excellence in the accounting field.

name: American Society for Biochemistry and Molecular Biology, UW-Madison Student Chapter
summary: Members of ASBMB are able to connect, share ideas, talk science, and have some fun while doing it! ASBMB is dedicated to providing pre-professional advising to its students as well as information through a variety of different seminars and meetings.

name: Wisconsin Alumni Student Board
summary: WASB's mission is to uphold our commitment to UW-Madison and the Wisconsin Alumni Association by representing, promoting and advancing its spirits, values and ideals through enriching the campus experience of all Badgers past, present and future.

name: Graduate Women in Science-Madison Chapter
summary: Graduate Women in Science is a local chapter of a national organization whose mission is to advance women's participation and recognition in science and foster research through grants, awards, and fellowships.

name: Wisconsin Space Program
summary: Wisconsin Space Program is the premier organization for students interested in rocketry and aerospace engineering, providing members with professional development and hands-on experience through team competitions.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers at UW-Madison
summary: ASCE focuses on supplementing students’ coursework with professional engineering experiences and is committed to promoting fellowship among students in the Civil and Environmental Engineering Department.

name: Human Factors and Ergonomics Society, University of Wisconsin-Madison Student Chapter
summary: The purpose of the Human Factors and Ergonomics Society University of Wisconsin-Madison Student Chapter is to promote knowledge concerning the characteristics of human beings that are applicable to the design of systems and devices of all kinds.

name: WUD Volunteer Action Committee
summary: WUD Volunteer Action centers on creating meaningful service opportunities right here on campus. Through pop-up events & hands-on projects, students can make an immediate impact in their community while building connections and learning through teamwork.

name: WUD Global Connections
summary: The committee strives to build awareness of global issues and cultures to students and staff here at UW-Madison through our fun and informative programming.

name: WUD Art Committee
summary: The WUD Art Committee is responsible for programming shows and exhibitions across three campus galleries. This student-run group manages every aspect of exhibitions - from artist selection and installation to art education and marketing.

name: Investment Banking Club
summary: A select group of talented students. We conduct weekly meetings with a focus on Investment Banking interview training, valuation training, discussion of current events, and networking trips to New York City and Chicago. www.wiscibc.com

name: Criminal and Juvenile Justice Student Association
summary: To help students prepare for the Criminal Justice Certificate Program internship, share career planning and graduate school advice, and introduce various careers and volunteer opportunities in the criminal justice field.

name: Students Today Leaders Forever, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: We are a student run organization that works to foster motivated student leaders by offering engaging service projects on campus throughout the school year and community service road trips called the Pay It Forward Tours over spring break.

name: Graduate Business Association
summary: The Graduate Business Association (GBA) serves as the umbrella organization for MBA and Master's students at the Wisconsin School of Business, enhancing their experience and promoting inclusion through academic, athletic, social, and service events.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers: University of Wisconsin-Madison Chapter
summary: Our mission at IISE UW–Madison is to enhance the ISyE student experience by connecting classroom learning with real-world application through professional development, industry exposure, leadership, and community.

name: Eta Kappa Nu - Theta Chapter
summary: Eta Kappa Nu is an honor society for the fields of Electrical and Computer Engineering.  Recently merged with IEEE, HKN is now the official honor society of IEEE.

name: Women's Law Student Association
summary: The Women’s Law Student Association (WLSA) promotes the advancement of women and non-binary people in the legal profession. WLSA sponsors panel discussions on women in the legal workplace, social events, and community service projects.

name: Pre-Law Society at UW - Madison
summary: The Pre-Law Society (PLS) at UW-Madison is a student organization that seeks to provide opportunities for similarly interested students to learn about the legal profession, the law school admissions process, and the LSAT.

name: University Physics Society
summary: Our organization's purpose is to bring together those interested in physics and provide them with information, opportunities, and an environment to aid in their studies, research, and future involving physics.

name: Wisconsin Black Student Union
summary: The Wisconsin Black Student Union (WBSU) is designed to foster an environment that better suits the needs of Black students and stands behind the philosophy that through utilizing our talents, we can make our presence felt on UW-Madison's campus.

name: Indigenous Law Student Association
summary: The Indigenous Law Student Association (ILSA) exists to provide an organization and forum for University of Wisconsin Law School students, while contributing to the advancement of Indigenous peoples.

name: Society of American Archivists-Student Chapter
summary: This organization is for students interested in the archival profession to connect and facilitate involvement with local, state, and national archives and archival associations.

name: Student Veterans of America-UW Madison
summary: SVA-UW is a coalition of military-affiliated students and faculty fostering a community focused on the transition from service to academics and bridging the military-civilian divide through camaraderie, education, and awareness.

name: BadgerThon
summary: BadgerThon engages UW–Madison students and the local community to promote children’s health and happiness! We raise funds and awareness for Children’s Wisconsin through events, fundraising, and partnerships. All proceeds support Children’s Wisconsin!

name: Psychology Club
summary: The Psychology Club at the University of Wisconsin-Madison is the ultimate resource hub for anyone interested in Psychology! Connect with faculty and professors, build a social and professional network, and head into the world one step ahead!

name: National Lawyers Guild - UW and Socialist Law Students of Wisconsin
summary: NLG & SocLaw - UW is an organization dedicated to the need for basic change in the structure of our political and economic system, aiming to bring together those at UW Law school who look upon the law as an instrument for the protection of the people.

name: Phi Upsilon Omicron - Nu Chapter
summary: Phi Upsilon Omicron, an honor society in the integrated field of family and consumer sciences, offers an environment empowering lifelong learning, leadership building, and ethical and scholastic excellence.

name: Construction Engineering Club
summary: The Construction Club was established to serve the students in the Construction Engineering and Management program. The primary goal of the Construction Club is to foster contacts between UW engineering students and industry professionals.

name: WUD Film Committee
summary:
WUD Film is the student-run programming club dedicated to bringing a diverse and exciting selection of films to the UW-Madison campus and surrounding community.

We meet on Mondays at 7 pm at Union South.

name: American Meteorological Society, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: The American Meteorological Society promotes the development and dissemination of information and education on the atmospheric and related oceanic and hydrologic sciences and the advancement of their professional applications.

name: Undergraduate Genetics Association
summary: The Undergraduate Genetics Association (UGA) provides students with academic, professional, and social opportunities within the genetics field. UGA promotes networking and peer mentoring, fosters academic excellence, and enhances interest in research.

name: Wisconsin International Studies Major Association
summary: Wisconsin International Studies Major Association (WISMA) aims to unite undergraduate students with a passion for global affairs, cultural diversity, and international studies. Students can be declared or interested in the IS major.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers UW-Madison Student Chapter
summary: The SHPE (UW-Madison) objective is to establish a forum for Hispanic professionals in STEM who serve as role models in the Hispanic community. We are brought together by heritage, social responsibility, and a desire to improve the equality of all people.

name: Society of Plastics Engineers, University of Wisconsin Student Chapter
summary: The main purpose of the SPE Student Chapter is to provide the opportunity to learn about cutting-edge technology relating to plastics, plastics processing, and computer simulation.

name: Lao and Cambodian Student Association
summary: The Lao and Cambodian Student Association strives to build a cohesive community within students of all ethnicities interested in learning about Laotian and/or Cambodian culture.

name: Federalist Society, UW-Madison Law School Chapter
summary: The Federalist Society is a non-partisan conservative and libertarian organization dedicated to freedom, federalism, and judicial restraint.

name: AIESEC Madison
summary: AIESEC-Madison is part of a global youth organization that develops leadership capabilities through international leadership, exchange and internship programs. AIESEC is the world's largest youth-led org with over 100,000 members in 130 countries

name: Theta Tau Professional Engineering Fraternity - Xi Chapter
summary: Theta Tau is the nation's oldest and largest professional engineering fraternity. Here at UW-Madison, our organization is open to anyone pursuing an engineering major - we are co-ed!

name: Hollywood Badgers
summary: Hollywood Badgers is the student chapter of the Los Angeles-based alumni group. We are on campus to help prepare students to work in all facets of the entertainment industry.

name: Society of Women Engineers
summary: The Society of Women Engineers (SWE), founded in 1950, is a not-for-profit educational and service organization. SWE is the driving force that establishes engineering as a highly desirable career aspiration.

name: Graduate Women in Business
summary: GWIB strives to provide a supportive network with the goal of advancing the promotion of women in business. All female-identifying MBA students are automatically members and all other graduate students of the University of Wisconsin are invited to join.

name: Pre-Optometry Club - UW-Madison
summary: The mission of the Pre-Optometry Club is to facilitate and educate students in their pursuit of a career in Optometry.

name: Wisconsin Public Interest Law Foundation, Inc.
summary: The Wisconsin Public Interest Law Foundation, Inc. ("PILF") is a student-run organization at the University of Wisconsin Law School committed to supporting law students who wish to pursue a career in public interest law.

name: Paleontology Club
summary: Paleontology Club is a student run organization in association with the UW-Madison Geology Museum open to all UW-Madison students with an interest in paleontology. Open to undergraduate and graduate students of all majors or disciplines.

name: Pi Tau Sigma-Wisconsin Alpha Chapter
summary: Pi Tau Sigma is an international honor society of top Juniors and Seniors in Mechanical Engineering.  Our four basic goals are to foster high ideals,  stimulate and support departmental activities, promote professional welfare, and  develop leadership.

name: Quarter Scale Tractor Team
summary: The Quarter scale tractor team builds a tractor each year for the international quarter scale competition during early summer in Peoria IL

name: Euchre Club - UW-Madison
summary:
To enjoy the game of euchre and teach anyone willing to learn.
We meet on one night a week in Engineering Hall from 7:00-9:00pm (check our weekly email for specific room assignments, but generally Room 2317). Day of week TBD from officers.

name: Family Medicine Interest Group, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: The mission of FMIG is to excite, promote, and support student interest in the specialty through active learning, mentorship, and exposure to the scope of Family Medicine.

name: National Alliance on Mental Illness, University of Wisconsin-Madison
summary: Our goals are to educate, advocate, and support students on matters of mental health. We educate by spreading the word about the reality of mental health. We advocate by trying to reduce the stigma of mental illness. We also provide peer support!

name: Geography Club
summary: Geography Club brings together students interested in geography in an informal setting. Through Club activities and social networks, students see how geography plays a major role in the lives of everyone and how geography can lead to a rewarding job.

name: Peer Learning Association - UW Madison
summary: PLA establishes and runs free weekly guided study group sessions for select courses. We work with interested faculty to ensure that students receive a more in-depth understanding of their course material. Interested in joining? Contact us via email.

name: Engineering Exposition
summary: Engineering EXPO is a group of students dedicated to successfully planning Engineering Exposition in April, 2026. Our goal is to open the doors of the College of Engineering to the community and inspire students to join the next generation of engineers.

name: Finance & Investment Society
summary: FIS seeks to increase students’ awareness and appreciation of the finance industry. The organization is designed to provide members with exposure to multiple disciplines in finance and foster educational opportunities outside the classroom,

name: Indian Students Association
summary: Indian Students Association hosts social, educational, cultural, and professional events to promote awareness and facilitate interaction with Indian culture and excellence.

name: Plant Sciences Graduate Student Council
summary: The PSGSC was created to foster community across plant sciences, including graduate students of Agroecology, Agronomy, Botany, Entomology, Horticulture, Plant Breeding & Plant Genetics, Plant Pathology, iBio, and Soil Science training programs.

name: Art History GradForum
summary: GradForum serves art historians at UW-Madison by sponsoring events including lectures, workshops, receptions, and professional opportunities, collaborating across departments and institutions, and developing community and camaraderie among grad students.

name: Black Law Students Association-UW Law School
summary: The Black law Students Association at the University of Wisconsin (BLSA-UW) is committed to providing a forum for all law students to engage in a dialogue regarding the needs of the Black law student and Black community.

name: Fundamentally Sound - Lower Voiced A Cappella
summary: Fundamentally Sound seeks to foster an environment which allows members to grow first as people and second as musicians. We focus on communication, teamwork, leadership, and camaraderie while sharing a unique brand of a cappella music with the community.

name: QLaw (Queer Law Students Association)
summary: QLaw is a law student organization dedicated to advocating for the lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer, intersex, ace (LGBTQIA+) community at the University of Wisconsin Law School.

name: American Society of Landscape Architects, Wisconsin Student Chapter
summary: American Society of Landscape Architects Wisconsin Student Chapter (WI-ASLA Student Chapter) supports students in Landscape Architecture and related fields by providing academic, professional, and social resources.

name: Phi Alpha Theta at UW-Madison
summary: Phi Alpha Theta is a professional society whose purpose is to promote the study of history through the encouragement of research, good teaching, publication, and the exchange of learning and ideas among historians.

name: Madison Business Mentoring
summary: Madison Business Mentoring (MBM) helps students prepare for the business world by pairing students with a professional mentor who is a UW graduate.

name: German and Dutch Graduate Student Association
summary: The GDGSA is a student association for graduate students of German and Dutch at the University of Wisconsin-Madison. We foster professional development and serve as an outreach organization for German and Dutch culture.

name: Undergraduate Neuroscience Society
summary: The purpose of UNS is to expand the amount of interest and knowledge about the neurological sciences in a fun, rewarding, and informal group setting.  All majors are welcome!

name: La Alianza
summary: La Alianza (f/k/a: LLSA) provides a friendly atmosphere designed to encourage participation in public service, assist other Latinos in the community at large, and promote unity amongst the entire UW Law School community, regardless of cultural background

name: La Follette School Student Association
summary: The members of LSSA are students enrolled in any of the degree programs of the La Follette School. The purpose of LSSA is to maintain and improve the academic, social, and professional qualities of life for students of the Robert M. La Follette School.

name: African Students Association
summary: ASA was formed to promote cultural and social awareness of the African people amongst the entire student and faculty of UW-Madison, to facilitate better communication and understanding between African people and all members of the university community.

name: Wisconsin Journal of Law, Gender & Society
summary: WJLGS exists to promote discussion of timely issues relating to law, gender, and society.

name: Social Work Student Union
summary: The Social Work Student Union is open to any students in the UW-Madison School of Social Work. Our mission is to unite student voices through advocacy, direct action, and rapport-building to ensure students receive a high-quality, equitable education.

name: SPIE/Optica Student Chapter at UW-Madison
summary: Our main goal is to promote awareness of Optical Science and Photonics on campus through organized seminars and discussions, to disseminate knowledge of the field of Optical Engineering, and to further the professional development of its students.

name: Redefined
summary: Redefined A Cappella is a mixed voice group known for their signature red accents and terrific sounds. The group performs popular songs with their voices alone with an ever-changing setlist of modern hits and classic throwbacks.

name: Capital Management Club
summary:
We are a student-run investment club focused on educating members about the equity research process to help individuals select attractive investment opportunities and prepare for careers in finance.

NOTE: Visit https://www.cmc-uwmadison.com to join

name: Filipinx American Student Organization
summary: FASO aims to educate and spread awareness about Filipinx culture & history, foster friendships, identify with one another, and work towards a common goal. FASO is based on the spirit of Bayanihan; the values of self-identity, family, and community.

name: Diversity in Business
summary: The Diversity in Business club intends to shape Graduate Student Business Professionals towards building diverse and inclusive business environments by creating well rounded, socially and culturally conscious leaders of Tomorrow.

name: Chi Omega - Nu Chapter
summary: Chi Omega is an intergenerational women's organization forever committed to our founding purposes: • Friendship • Personal integrity • Service to others • Academic excellence • Community/campus involvement •Personal and career development

name: Student Association of Taiwan
summary: The Student Association of Taiwan (SAT) was established to provide a platform where students from both Taiwan and around the world can meet, support, and make friends with one another.

name: Women in Business
summary: WIB strives to create a network of empowered women in business who not only feel supported in their professional development, but personally through a community of other members as well.

name: Graduate Marketing Network
summary: The Graduate Marketing Network (GMN) is for Wisconsin MBA students who love marketing.  Our goal is to foster knowledge and build excitement of marketing across all Wisconsin MBA specializations and help prepare students for successful careers.

name: Bucky Pre-Health Volunteering Association
summary: To provide UW-Madison students with volunteering opportunities within the Madison community. BVA is an organization dedicated to bridging students who have interests in pursuing a career in the health field.

name: Applied Linguistics Students Association
summary: We are a group of graduate students of English Language and Linguistics, Applied English Linguistics, and other graduate students, interested in language and linguistics at the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

name: Korean Business Student Association
summary: KBSA is a student organization registered at the Wisconsin School of Business. Our organization aims to provide supplementary education about the business environment and help facilitate a successful transition into the professional world.

name: Global Health Alliance at UW-Madison
summary: GHA at UW is part of a national network of university chapters working to create a social movement for global health equity. We accomplish this through forming both local and international partnerships, and educational programs.

name: Arnold Air Society/Silver Wings - Richard Ira Bong Squadron
summary: Arnold Air Society & Silver Wings are national sister organizations, working together to develop future leaders through community service, professional development, and education about our national defense.

name: Aspiring Educators of Wisconsin, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: Aspiring Educators of Wisconsin is a pre-professional association for those pursuing careers in the field of education.

name: Technology and Intellectual Property Student Organization
summary: The purpose of TIPSO is to provide a forum for law students to learn about the study and practice of intellectual property and technology law.

name: Southeast Asia Research Group
summary: A student research forum designed to facilitate the professional and intellectual development of students whose work engages with Southeast Asia

name: Student Chapter of The Wildlife Society (UW-Madison)
summary: The Wildlife Society isn't just about preparing students for careers in wildlife (though that's definitely a big part!) it's also about making better stewards of the earth out of all of us, and having a ton of fun doing so!

name: Beta Theta Pi Fraternity-Alpha Pi Chapter
summary: Beta Theta Pi is dedicated to developing men of principle for a principled life through our commitment to the cultivation of the intellect, mutual assistance, trust, integrity, and responsible conduct among our members.

name: Children's Justice Project
summary: Children's Justice Project brings together people interested in promoting justice for children and juveniles, including the rights of children and juveniles in the legal, educational, health care, and social services systems.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America - UW-Madison
summary: Young Democratic Socialists of America (YDSA) is the student section of the Democratic Socialists of America (DSA), the largest socialist political organization in the United States.

name: Madison Misnomer, The
summary:
Premier satire publication on campus
@MadisonMisnomer

name: Wisconsin Engineer Magazine
summary: Our focus is to create an award-winning, informative, professional and entertaining student-run magazine and website focused on engineering, science, and technology topics for the UW-Madison community.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma - Eta Chapter
summary: Kappa Kappa Gamma is an organization of women, which seeks friendship, mutual support, opportunities for self-growth, respect for intellectual development, and an understanding of and an allegiance to positive ethical principles.

name: Quiz Bowl - UW-Madison
summary: Quiz Bowl is an organization dedicated to knowledge competition.  We compete on the local and national level against other schools in a variety of academic and non-academic (trivia) quiz tournaments.

name: Second Language Acquisition Student Organization
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to provide a forum in which individuals interested in second language acquisition or second language learning/teaching may engage in meaningful social, academic, and professional discussions.

name: Real Estate Club
summary: The Real Estate Club is the largest club for students interested in the world of commercial real estate. Open to both graduate and undergraduate students, the club meets several times per semester to learn from a variety of real estate professionals.

name: Kaleidoscope Graduate Student Conference
summary: The Organizing Committee of the Kaleidoscope Graduate Student Conference at UW-Madison is the organization responsible for the planning and execution of an annual conference.

name: Badgers with a Heart
summary: Badgers with a Heart is a UW–Madison student org dedicated to inclusion and awareness for people with disabilities. We partner with the Autism Society to support children with autism and show the compassion and impact of UW students in Madison.

name: Socialist Alternative UW Madison
summary: Socialist Alternative is a socialist organization fighting for the rights of workers and students against racism, sexism, homophobia, war and capitalism. We are revolutionary socialists fighting for ordinary people to have control of the economy.

name: Slavic Graduate Student Organization
summary: The Slavic GSO provides a forum for graduate students in the UW Slavic program to express concerns or ideas, participate in professional development workshops, participate in academic colloquiums, and organize events for/about the Slavic community.

name: MBA Entrepreneurship Association
summary: The Entrepreneurship Association is a student organization open to graduate students who are interested in entrepreneurship.

name: Music Teachers National Association Collegiate Chapter - UW Madison
summary: Our student chapter is committed to building a stronger link between the University and the community, while continuing to invest in our own development as musicians and teachers in order to model the highest artistic values and professional standards.

name: Student Academy of Audiology - UW Chapter
summary: The SAA - UW Chapter is a professional student organization dedicated to advancing the education and training of future audiologists. We provide education about hearing and balance, in addition to promoting audiology in the local community.

name: Business and Tax Law Association
summary: BATLaw (Business and Tax Law Association) is the Law School student organization dedicated to providing information and support to law students who contemplate or merely wish to know more about business law and/or business law careers.

name: Association of Computing Machinery-Women (UW-Madison Chapter)
summary: The student chapter of ACM-W, ACM’s Women in Computing, serving students at UW-Madison. We provide social, educational and outreach activities for women in the Computer Sciences department, in the university, and in the surrounding community.

name: Wisconsin Student Planning Association
summary: To provide students of the Department of Planning and Landscape Architecture with a welcoming social, professional, and academic network through which the free exchange of ideas may flourish.

name: American Chemical Society, UW-Madison Student Chapter
summary: The American Chemical Society Student Chapter at the University of Wisconsin promotes the learning of chemistry for students in all divisions of chemistry and related majors by supporting their academic and professional development and research pursuits.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
summary: APALSA is an academic, career, and social support network aimed at enhancing APIDA law students' experiences. We strive to create a safe, supportive, inclusive space so our members can find the community, support and resources they seek to succeed.

name: InterMission Theatre
summary: United by our passion for storytelling, InterMission Theatre (IMT) empowers students to present performing arts through interdisciplinary collaboration.

name: Change Makers Collective
summary: CMC-UW is an academic student organization that operates beyond the classroom by offering further personal and professional development experiences. The organization prepares members for success and fulfillment in service-oriented sectors.

name: Amnesty International at UW-Madison
summary: Amnesty International at UW-Madison is a non-partisan human rights group that is dedicated to ending human rights abuses worldwide.  We aim to raise awareness, hold events, and take action to end violations of human rights on our campus and beyond!

name: iQueery
summary: A group for iSchool LGBTQIA+ and allied students to promote community.

name: Mentorship Achievement Program
summary: MAP mentors are UW health students, committed to providing the support and encouragement necessary to help disadvantaged middle schoolers in the Madison area realize their potential.  Volunteer to become a MAP mentor today!

name: American Constitution Society, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: The American Constitution Society,  UW-Madison Chapter (ACS) features progressive UW law students committed to using legal ideas and Constitutional scholarship to shape law and policy for the social good.

name: TEDxUWMadison
summary: TEDxUWMadison aims to pursue TED's mission "broadening horizons" and plan a TEDx event each year. Bringing together technology, entertainment and design, TED holds annual conferences that gather experts in various fields and shares their ideas at ted.com

name: Badger Clean Snowmobile Team- IC
summary: The Clean Snowmobile team designs, builds and competes in the internal combustion class of the Advanced Snowmobile Challenge.

name: Graduate Association of Medieval Studies
summary: GAMS is a graduate student collective for students interested in Medieval Studies. GAMS is open to graduate students from all departments.

name: Math Club
summary: Math Club provides a space for students interested in mathematics to connect with each other through academic talks and social events.

name: Trans-Asia Graduate Student Association
summary: TAGSA will plan and implement an annual graduate student conference at UW-Madison to provide an opportunity for graduate students to present their Asia-related research from a variety of trans-disciplinary backgrounds.

name: African Cultural Studies Student Association
summary: African Cultural Studies Student Association encourages members to engage in academic and social activities that strengthen their social and intellectual cohesion and enhances the visibility of the Department of African Cultural Studies

name: Badger Consulting
summary: Badger Consulting is a 360 degree consulting firm at the University of Wisconsin-Madison, which is operated by students who work together to revamp and improve Madison area small businesses in the fields of finance, marketing, and research & development.

name: Transcend UW
summary: Transcend is UW's hub for project-development and innovation on campus. Whether you're in a startup or just simply into revolutionary ideas, we want you! We're a community of thinkers, hackers and entrepreneurs who make new products a reality.

name: Geoscience Graduate Student Association
summary: The GGSA is an organization representing the graduate students in the Geoscience Dept and exists to organize department functions and as an official go-between for the students to the faculty/staff.

name: Women In Science and Engineering - Out of House
summary: WISE Out of House Members connects the Women in Science and Engineering Learning Community Residents and Alumni to student across the campus to better each other and our community.

name: Partners In Health | Engage, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: The purpose of PIH | Engage is to raise funds in support for Partners in Health, advocate for health policies that promotes global health equity, and educate our PIH | Engage community and the local Madison community about global health issues.

name: American Marketing Association (UW-Madison Collegiate Chapter)
summary: AMA is a professional association for students interested in marketing to collaborate and grow as leaders and professionals in the field through case competitions, networking, corporate treks, executive panels, community outreach, and socials.

name: Wisconsin Law Review
summary: The Wisconsin Law Review is a student-run journal of legal analysis and commentary that is used by professors, judges, practitioners, and others researching contemporary legal topics.

name: Graduate Student Association of the University of Wisconsin-Madison Department of Atmospheric and Oceanic Sciences
summary: The Graduate Student Organization (GSA) of the AOS department at UW-Madison acts as a body to facilitate communication between the graduate students and AOS faculty members.

name: Alexander Hamilton Society UW-Madison Chapter
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society (AHS) is an independent, non-partisan, not-for-profit organization dedicated to promoting constructive debate on basic principles and contemporary issues in foreign, economic, and national security policy.

name: Turning Point USA Chapter at UW-Madison
summary: Turning Point USA at UW-Madison is a nonpartisan political organization dedicated to restoring American greatness by advocating classical values of free speech, limited government, and fiscal responsibility.

name: Graduate Student Association for the English Department
summary: The Graduate Student Association (GSA) is a body of students  from all five areas of the UW-Madison English department: Literary Studies, Composition and Rhetoric, English Language and Linguistics, Creative Writing, and Interdisciplinary Theatre Studies.

name: Society for Ecological Restoration Student Association: UW-Madison
summary: SERUWM is an organization focused on ecological restoration in our community and world-wide. SERUWM connects students, faculty, experts, and related groups to restoration projects, activities, speakers, research and resources.

name: Sifting and Winnowing
summary: Sifting and Winnowing is an academic organization that supports student involvement in public policy, law, and political science. We plan an annual Ideas Conference at UW-Madison and partner with campus organizations to discuss policy.

name: Student Chapter of the Association for Computing Machinery at UW-Madison
summary: SACM is the student chapter of the Association for Computing Machinery at the University of Wisconsin. See the full description for more info. Please do not send us messages through WIN. To contact the officers, send mail to sacm@cs.wisc.edu.

name: Wildlife Disease Association, UW-Madison Student Chapter
summary: Our mission is to introduce UW-Madison students to the world of wildlife and disease through opportunities in disease identification, career exploration, and exciting new adventures within the Madison area.

name: Aspiring Physician Assistant Association
summary: Student organization designed to aid, prepare, and connect those who desire to be a physician assistant by providing knowledge, insight, and resources necessary for a well rounded undergraduate career while preparing members for PA school.

name: Korean-American Scientists and Engineers Association Wisconsin YG Chapter
summary: KSEA offers young scientists and engineers with opportunities and connections throughout the world, especially in the US, Canada, and South Korea. We hope to gain greater interdependence between all levels of scholars.

name: Sheepshead Club - UW Madison
summary: A community for people to play and learn Sheepshead; a popular midwestern card game. No experience is needed to join. There will be opportunities to learn Sheepshead and have a good time playing with others who love the game.

name: Alpha Xi Delta, Theta Chapter
summary: Alpha Xi Delta is a progressive organization dedicated to the personal growth of women. We emphasize the ideals our Founders instilled in our Fraternity: Sisterhood, leadership, knowledge and service to our communities.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law Chapter
summary: The Phi Alpha Delta Pre-Law program aims to help students learn more about the legal field. PAD is open to all who express interest and provides access to networking opportunities and resources from our international organization.

name: National Organization for the Professional Advancement of Black Chemists and Chemical Engineers, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: This organization promotes the academic excellence and professional development of undergraduate, graduate, and post-doctorate students in chemistry, chemical engineering, and related fields.

name: Asian Musical Instruments Community
summary: The purpose of Asian Musical Instruments Community is to bring students and UW affiliates from different ethnic and social backgrounds to engage in discussion about music genres from the Asian continent and related topics from a global perspective.

name: American Institute of Graphic Arts UW-Madison
summary: AIGA UW-Madison is a local student group tied to a national organization for graphic designers. AIGA UW-Madison provides opportunities to learn more about the graphic design community locally and nationally.

name: Delight Ministries, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: Delight is a college community that grows together, serves together, learns together, and does life together while chasing the heart of God. We gather weekly to discuss a chapter from Delight's semester curriculum designed to help you encounter Jesus.

name: Government Law Society
summary: The Government Law Society (GovLaw) is a law student organization that provides students interested in state, local, federal, and tribal government law with opportunities to further their knowledge of these subjects and advance their careers.

name: Badger Solar Racing
summary: Badger Solar Racing is a student-led team that designs, builds, and races a solar powered vehicle in the American Solar Challenge (ASC), a biannual race across the US, and the Formula Sun Grand Prix (FSGP), an annual race on a closed-loop track.

name: WiscWind
summary: This multi-disciplinary team of students competes in the US Department of Energy’s Collegiate Wind Competition (CWC) to build a small-scale prototype wind turbine, conduct outreach in the local community, and develop a detailed wind farm proposal.

name: Wealth Management Group
summary: WMG is a student-led community passionate about wealth management, offering career development, exclusive recruiting trips, and direct access to top firms through networking opportunities to top cities.

name: Women in Economics
summary: Women in Economics (WE) advocate for and empower women in the field of economics. WE strive to promote awareness of gender disparities and barriers within economics and to educate and improve the community.

name: Engineers for a Sustainable World (UW-Madison Chapter)
summary: Engineers for a Sustainable World (ESW) is a non-profit network committed to building a better world. ESW is comprised of students, university faculty, and professionals who are dedicated to building a more sustainable world.

name: Women in Scientific Education and Research
summary: WISER aims to foster relationships between students that identify as women and women researchers on campus. Not only does WISER stimulate campus-wide research, but it also creates a community that promotes networking, mentoring, and community engagement.

name: Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS): University of Wisconsin - Madison Chapter
summary: The student chapter of INFORMS at the Operations Research is an association for students to engage with each other and share the knowledge of experience and research in operations research.

name: American Society of Heating, Refrigerating, and Air-Conditioning Engineers - UW-Madison Chapter
summary: ASHRAE explores the engineering industry of heating, ventilation, air-conditioning, and refrigeration. It strives to  support sustainability and connect members with professionals in this area.

name: Cardiac on Campus
summary: Cardiac on Campus aims to prevent sudden cardiac arrest at UW–Madison and in the broader Madison community. We achieve this through free CPR classes, free electrocardiogram (ECG) screenings, and donations of AEDs to campus and local buildings.

name: Journal of Undergraduate Science and Technology
summary: The Journal of Undergraduate Science and Technology (JUST) is the only peer-reviewed research and science communication journal on campus. JUST is a bi-annually published journal, written and administered by undergraduates.

name: Bundle Up
summary: A service organization dedicated to the collection and distribution of clothes and personal hygiene products to unhoused individuals in the Madison area.

name: Pi Kappa Phi - Kappa Lambda
summary: Pi Kappa Phi was founded on the premise of creating leadership opportunities for our members. Our mission is to create an uncommon and lifelong brotherhood that develops leaders and encourages service to others for the betterment of our communities.

name: Wisconsin NROTC
summary: Wisconsin's Naval ROTC program is committed to developing and training current college students to be commissioned Naval Officers in the United States Navy upon graduation.

name: French Club
summary: French Club is an organization that focuses its activities on French language and Francophone culture. We encourage anyone who is interested in learning about the French language/culture to join! We host social, volunteering, and fundraising events.

name: Insight Wisconsin
summary: Members engage in real-world innovative projects (both client-based and original idea-based) to develop meaningful and professional design experience, while working in diverse teams and cultivating a passionate learning environment.

name: Environmental Engineering Club
summary: The objective/purpose of this organization shall be to provide a platform for students interested in the field of environmental engineering to learn more about careers, network with professionals and get to know each other.

name: Big Brothers Big Sisters at the University of Wisconsin
summary: Big Brother Big Sisters at UW-Madison will serve as the student organization that Big Brothers Big Sisters can trust to help advocate their mission on campus through mentor recruiting, fundraising, and event planning.

name: Anatomy Club, UW-Madison
summary: The Anatomy Club is a student organization that seeks to assemble students interested in the field of anatomy and promote the application of knowledge pertaining to this field.

name: Chinese American Student Association
summary: CHASA strives to promote Chinese American culture on campus as well as to provide opportunities where students are able to build community through social events.

name: Artificial Intelligence Club, UW-Madison
summary: Committed to Artificial Intelligence education and research, the AI Club of UW-Madison is the community and resource centre for all things AI.

name: Helios UW-Madison
summary: Helios engages in climate action by implementing sustainable energy projects on campus to address the climate crisis and create a more livable future.

name: ALT Magazine
summary: ALT Magazine is a fashion magazine dedicated to unique and individualistic fashion with an emphasis on multiculturalism, social issues, and inclusion.

name: Gender Minorities and Women in Physics
summary: This is a group for people committed to the advancement of women and gender minorities in physics. Our goals are to provide both professional development and support for women and gender minorities in physics at every step in their careers.

name: Graduate Association of Philosophy
summary: To maintain and support an environment where graduate students of philosophy can support each other through all aspects of academic life including professional development, professional service, networking, and teaching.

name: Women in Real Estate
summary: We aspire to build a community of women wishing to pursue real estate careers and to connect these women with professionals, therefore promoting confidence and success in a male-dominated industry.

name: Political Science Student Association
summary: The purpose of The Political Science Student Association (PSSA) is to provide events, networking opportunities, and professional development for students interested or declared in the Political Science major or certificate.

name: Statistics Graduate Student Association
summary: The official student chapter at UW-Madison of the American Statistical Society, the world's largest professional community of statisticians. We help connect students interested in statistics to each other and to other statistical communities at large.

name: Succulent Club of UW-Madison
summary: This organization promotes the appreciation of succulents, cacti, and household plants.

name: War In Society and Culture Student Association
summary: This organization’s purpose is to sponsor academic, social and professional development opportunities for students in the relationship between armed conflict and the larger society.

name: Queen In You, UW Madison
summary: The purpose of Queen In You is to cater to the advancement of women of color in areas of professionalism, self-esteem, and self-development in the surrounding communities and on campus.

name: Mock Trial - Wisconsin Law
summary: Mock Trial - Wisconsin Law is a competitive trial advocacy program at the University of Wisconsin Law School.

name: Genetic Medicine Interest Group
summary: The goal of GMIG is to connect students with physicians and scientists in the fields of genetic and genomic medicine and to create opportunities for career exploration.

name: Labor and Employment Student Association
summary: LESA is a student association for law students to discuss labor and employment law and hear from professionals in the field.

name: What's Eating My Plants?
summary: WEMP is a student led outreach group. We host at least one outreach event a month to share knowledge of the plant sciences with communities in WI and beyond. Our mission is to increase scientific agency and access, especially in underserved communities.

name: Chicanx & Latinx Certificate Student Association
summary: ChiLaCSA - It is an academic association that is affiliated with the Chican@ and Latin@ Studies Program. ChiLaCSA serves as the certificate student's voice in the decision-making process in the Chican@ and Latin@ Studies Program.

name: Scabbard and Blade Society - 1A Mother Company
summary: We are dedicated to developing aspiring and current military officers that emulate the Five Star qualities while fostering strong joint-service relationships.

name: Phi Gamma Nu - Epsilon Theta Chapter
summary: The Epsilon Theta Chapter of Phi Gamma Nu produces leaders who are devoted to professionalism, service, and brotherhood. By encouraging entrepreneurship, we empower others to harness their passions while contributing to a strong community of students.

name: Wisconsin Waale A Cappella
summary: Wisconsin Waale is UW-Madison's premiere South Asian A cappella group.

name: Wisconsin Undergraduate Business Council
summary: The Wisconsin Undergraduate Business Council (UBC) is the official undergraduate student government of the Wisconsin School of Business, fostering a community where students feel connected, valued, respected, empowered, and equipped for success.

name: International & Comparative Education Research Group
summary: ICERG is a multidisciplinary community of UW-Madison faculty and graduate students dedicating to advancing scholarship in the field of comparative education.

name: Social and Environmental Business Advocates
summary: To build an inclusive and equitable culture in the Wisconsin School of Business, centered on social and environmental justice. We create intentional spaces for students to be heard and develop solutions together.

name: Diversity and Inclusion Association
summary: We are the Diversity and Inclusion Association (DIA). Our association strives to promote a diversity of perspectives into teaching, research, professional development, and community-building endeavors.

name: School Psychology Student Association
summary: The School Psychology Student Association (SPSA) is for graduate students currently enrolled in the School Psychology Program in the Department of Educational Psychology at UW–Madison.

name: Queer and Trans Engineers
summary: QTE, Queer and Trans Engineers, is a student organization functioning to support and provide a space for engineering students who identify as LGBTQIA+. We strive to uplift Q&T engineering students through advocacy, education and social events.

name: Data & Analytics Club
summary: The vision of Data & Analytics Club is to mitigate the gap between courses knowledge and industries practices. The mission is to prepare business badgers, who are interested in data analytics, for future career through various applied learning events.

name: Students Demand Action at UW Madison
summary: Students Demand Action is a nonpartisan group working to bring the power of advocacy, research, and grassroots organization to UW-Madison with the aim of educating and advocating about gun safety and gun violence prevention.

name: American Indian Science and Engineering Society (UW-Madison Chapter)
summary: AISES provides a space that elevates Native American and Indigenous students in STEM, while fostering an inclusive community open to all students who respect and support Indigenous voices in these fields.

name: Graduate Association of Interdisciplinary Neuroscience Students
summary: GAINS is a student association open to any graduate student associated with the NTP at the University of Wisconsin, Madison. Its function is to foster community, facilitate communication, organize events, and foster career development in the NTP.

name: Leukemia & Lymphoma Society at UW-Madison
summary: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society (LLS) seeks to cure leukemia, lymphoma, Hodgkin’s Disease, and myeloma, and improve the quality of life for patients through research, patient support, and advocacy.

name: Cinemadison
summary: Cinemadison is an award winning, student-led film production organization where UW-Madison film enthusiasts can come together to produce films in a collaborative environment.

name: American Cancer Society on Campus
summary: ACS On Campus is the American Cancer Society's program to empower students in our collective mission against this disease. Our work focusses on the four focus areas of ACS: Advocacy, Discovery, Patient Support and Fundraising.

name: Information Systems Society
summary: We take pride in fostering a dynamic community of students eager to explore careers at the intersection of technology and business by creating connections between students from various disciplines who share a passion for technology-driven opportunities.

name: Advocates for Immigrants' Rights
summary: The purpose of Advocates for Immigrants’ Rights (AIR) is to represent the interests of students advocating for immigrants’ rights through networking, community outreach, education, and mentorship.

name: Supply Chain & Operations Club
summary: Supply Chain & Operations Club(SCO) is a student group composed of students with an interest in Supply Chain and Operations management. The mission of the club is to provide students with an opportunity to get beyond pure classroom learning and interact.

name: Advocates for Rare Diseases
summary: Advocates for Rare Diseases is a student organization that helps raise awareness and funding for the thousands of rare diseases that go overlooked. The focus is to build recognition and understanding of these diseases while helping raise funds.

name: Delta Sigma Phi, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: The men of Delta Sigma Phi strive to become the Better Man by embodying the principles of culture, harmony, and friendship.

name: Women in Business Technology
summary: Women in Business Technology aims to introduce undergraduate women to careers within the technology industry by breaking down barriers while empowering one another through various speakers, networking events, and community service opportunities.

name: Women in STEM
summary: This club will create a community for women in STEM and will help to promote women in STEM-related fields.

name: Solis
summary: Women and Gender Minorities in Plasma Science

name: ColorStack at UW-Madison
summary: Space to support students of color studying computer science and other tech majors to engage in community building, academic support, and career development.

name: Theravada Studies Group UW–Madison
summary: The Theravada Studies Group UW–Madison aims to promote, enhance, and disseminate research related to Theravada Buddhist studies in disciplines and departments across the University, as well as in our local communities.

name: Aspiring Anesthesiologist Assistant Association
summary: The goal of AAAA is to provide support for pre-anesthesiologist assistant students by providing resources and community.

name: Poultry Science Club, UW-Madison
summary: Any UW student interested in poultry or looking to learn more about poultry is welcome to join! Our main activities include going to the International Products and Processing Expo (IPPE), poultry farm tours, and selling specialty products.

name: Perfect Pair UW-Madison
summary: Volunteer based organization that connects current UW-Madison students with residents at assisted living facilities throughout the Madison area. Residents and students are matched based on specific interests and hobbies.

name: Neuroscience Book Club
summary: Also called the Brainy Book Club, we're a group undergraduates interested in learning more about neuroscience through journals and podcasts. We meet every other week during the semester. Follow our Instagram and join our GroupMe for meeting updates.

name: Antiracism Learning and Action in Neuroscience
summary: We are an integrative group of neuroscientists seeking to further our antiracist impact through education and action at the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

name: Runway of Dreams UW-Madison
summary: Runway of Dreams UW-Madison is a non-profit organization that works toward a future of inclusion, acceptance, and opportunity in the fashion industry for people with disabilities.

name: Graduate Engineering Mechanics Society
summary: An organization for graduate students in Engineering Mechanics and Mechanical Engineering which is designed to welcome incoming students, enable networking, and share research, advice, and resources amongst them.

name: Real Estate and Securities Investment Law Society
summary: This society seeks to build a bridge between law students and those who can offer academic and career advice in the real estate and securities law sectors.

name: Biological Systems Engineering Graduate Student Organization
summary: Biological Systems Engineering Graduate Student Organization (BSE GSO) a group for grad students and postdocs in BSE and related fields!

name: CHE Graduate Associate Organizing Committee
summary: Graduate Associate Organizing Committee of the Center for Culture, History, and Environment (CHE)

name: Cardinal Trading Group
summary: CTG aims to provide education, networking resources, and talent development for our members. Together, we develop algorithmically driven trading strategies typically employed by modern hedge funds to gain an edge on financial markets.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. Epsilon Theta Chapter
summary:
The Epsilon Theta Chapter of Omega Psi Phi Fraternity Incorporated.
Chartered September 15, 1969
Madison, Wisconsin

name: The Issue
summary: The Issue is a UW-Madison student-led creative collective producing curated art, fashion, media, and culture. It is a compilation of creative student work in various mediums which responds to matters relevant to students on campus and beyond.

name: Leaders Igniting Transformation
summary: We’re a Black and Brown-led nonprofit, organizing young people to build independent political power for social, racial, & economic justice, engaging in electoral organizing, direct action, public policy advocacy, & leadership development.

name: Wisconsin AI Safety Initiative
summary: At WAISI, we think that reducing risks from advanced artificial intelligence may be one of the most important problems of our time. It's a challenge that spans technical research, policy, and ethics — and we're bringing people together to work on it.

name: Vault: Business Behind Fashion, The
summary: The Vault is an organization that explores the intersection of business, technology, and design in fashion. We educate members on behind-the-scenes careers and provide hands-on experiences, networking, and service opportunities across these industries.

name: Wisconsin Business Review
summary: The Wisconsin Business Review serves as the pen and paper for those who talk and think business. We're platformed to deliver coverage on contemporary business issues, publish student commentary on such issues, and more.

name: Student Voters Union
summary: Student Voters Union is a nonpartisan organization of UW-Madison students and representatives of student organizations whose purpose is to expand student voter access, break down barriers to voting, and educate students on voting and elections.

name: Biokind Analytics, University of Wisconsin-Madison Chapter
summary: Biokind Analytics brings together undergraduate data scientists and statisticians across the world to apply classroom learning for meaningful, positive societal impact in their communities.

name: Somali Student Association
summary: The Somali Student Association at UW-Madison fosters an inclusive community for Somali & non-Somali UW-Madison students, faculty, and staff. Engaging events and gatherings offer an immersive experience in the vibrant world of Somali culture.

name: Association of Latino Professionals For America, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: The mission of the ALPFA at UW Madison is to empower and develop the Latine community as leaders in the business world. We strive to provide a platform for networking, professional development, mentorship, and community engagement.

name: Doctoral Student Organization: Sandra Rosenbaum School of Social Work
summary: The DSO-SRSSW is an organization intended for all students currently enrolled in the Social Welfare PhD program at the University of Wisconsin-Madison.

name: Mechanical Engineering Graduate Student Advisory Council
summary: The purpose of the ME GSAC is to improve graduate student life in the Department of Mechanical Engineering. We advocate for graduate students, organize social and professional development events, and support a more inclusive environment.

name: Wisconsin Urban Planning Association
summary: WUPA is a social and professional organization for all students interested in urban planning topics such as architecture, sociology, sustainability, and more. WUPA provides opportunities for members to build community with peers and DPLA faculty.

name: Strategies for Ecology, Education, Diversity, and Sustainability(UW-Madison)
summary: SEEDS (Strategies for Ecology, Education, Diversity, and Sustainability) is an initiative aimed at promoting diversity and inclusivity within the field of ecology by creating opportunities for underrepresented groups in ecology and environmental sciences

name: Business in Medicine
summary: An organization looking at exploring all aspects of business as it relates to medicine and the healthcare field as a whole.

name: Corporate Finance Club
summary: The purpose of the Corporate Finance Club is to give students exposure to the world of corporate finance through a wide variety of speaker events, professional development workshops, career treks, case competitions, and much more.

name: Wisconsin Undergraduate Law Review
summary: The Wisconsin Undergraduate Law Review works to provide unique peer reviewed publishing opportunities for undergraduate students interested in law.

name: International Society for Pharmaceutical Engineering (UW-Madison Chapter)
summary: ISPE serves its members by leading scientific, technical, and regulatory advancement throughout the entire pharmaceutical lifecycle. We promote interactions between students and industry professionals in the field of pharmaceutical engineering.

name: We Outside
summary: We Outside is a BIPOC student-led organization intended to connect BIPOC students and reclaim the place of marginalized students at UW-Madison in natural spaces.

name: Ethiopian and Eritrean Student Association
summary: The mission of the Ethiopian-Eritrean Student Association is to uplift the wellbeing and presence of the rich and varied cultures of Ethiopia and Eritrea. Our organization serves to benefit the overall development of out members in all spheres.

name: Campus Craft Club
summary: Campus Craft Club is an organization that brings people together through the shared interest of doing crafts. We aim to create a stress free zone where students can relax by doing provided crafts or bringing projects they are working on.

name: Women in Electrical and Computer Engineering
summary: Women in Electrical and Computer Engineering aims to bring together and support women within the department of Electrical and Computer Engineering.

name: Strato Venture Capital
summary: The early-stage investment club at UW Madison.

name: Wisconsin Private Equity Club
summary: The Wisconsin Private Equity Club is an organization for individuals interested in the private equity and alternative investments industries. The club primarily focuses on member development, exposure to professionals, and recruitment success.

name: ASP Graduate Working Group
summary: This is a graduate research and writing group consisting of students who research interests implicate Africa and its diasporas.

name: Sigma Chi - Lambda
summary: We are a fraternity with a deep history at UW Madison. We were first established at this University in 1884 and have had a constant presence in the community since. We believe in the ideals of Friendship, Justice, and Learning.

name: Youth Involved
summary: An organization where the power of youth ignites change and uplifts communities. Committed to empowering students to combat economic strife and uplift the spirit of service through impactful events and community outreach.

name: RoboMaster UW-Madison
summary: RoboMaster UW-Madison is a student organization that gathers robotics engineers in mechanical, hardware, and software to compete in RoboMaster North America (RMNA) robotics competition.

name: Association of Individual Rock Bands
summary: The Association of Individual Rock Bands celebrates the creativity and energy of independent student rock bands by providing management and collective resources: performance opportunities and assistance during preparation.

name: Badgers in Themed Entertainment
summary: Inform the members of and provide opportunities to be involved with the themed entertainment industry, and the enthusiastic community at this university. The group will be focused on theme parks, amusement parks, and water parks.

name: Scholars in Education
summary: Scholars in Education strives to support students passionate about education while fostering a collaborative community among peers.

name: For the Femmes
summary: To support and create open forum spaces to share, relate, debrief, receive resources etc., allow women who are going into fields where they are the minority to be seen and heard, and experience minority women in a positive light.

name: Mu Epsilon Delta (University of Wisconsin - Madison Chapter)
summary: Mu Epsilon Delta - UW Chapter is a professional organization for individuals considering and embarking on a health career following graduation. We are committed to helping students succeed in their aspirations through our core principles and tenants.

name: Transracial Adoptees at the University of Wisconsin - Madison
summary: Adop-T. aims to create a safe and welcoming environment for adoptees and intersecting identities to share their experiences and join in the community. We strive to provide a space where members can engage in social, educational, and service activities.

name: Environmental Science Club, UW-Madison
summary: The Environmental Science Club brings together Badger students passionate about the environment & sustainability through environmental volunteering, social events, field trips, and bringing in guest speakers from relevant disciplines and professions.

name: Embracing Change through Hispanic/Latino Opportunities
summary: ECHO stands to focus on 3 pillars: Financial literacy for the community, pro-bono consulting services to Latino-owned companies, and immersing our members with opportunities and mentorship.

name: Student Body for Social Business
summary: SB² strives to be a catalyst for social change, through innovative ideas and entrepreneurial strategies. The goal is to implement social change that self-sustain and work to solve issues at their roots, bettering the communities around them.

name: Black Men Collective of UW-Madison
summary: The Black Men Collective of UW-Madison strives to foster and create a community for the minority of black men in higher education. We will achieve this goal by hosting events where members can develop socially, professionally, and emotionally.

name: Healthcare grOup CUriouS about Point of Care UltraSound
summary: We aim to provide hands-on training for healthcare professionals interested in exploring the use of point of care ultrasound technology. This will occur through organized workshops, webinars, and practical demonstrations.

name: ISyE Connections
summary: ISyE Connections is a club dedicated to connecting students interested in Industrial and Systems Engineering with a mentor who shares similar interests and aspirations. These mentors consist of upperclassmen, graduate students, and alumni in industry.

name: Historical Analysis of Medicine, Science, and Technology Engagement Raising
summary: The purpose of HAMSTER is to serve as an interest group bringing together all graduate students working on distant and contemporary histories of science, medicine, and technology, regardless of home department.

name: Water@UW Graduate Student Group
summary: This group is housed under Water@UW, with the goal to foster connection and communication among graduate students and postdoctoral scholars of the University of Wisconsin-Madison water community across disciplines.

name: Brain Exercise Initiative at The University of Wisconsin Madison
summary: We are a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that trains volunteers to use simple math, writing, and reading-aloud exercises as an intervention to improve cognitive function in those with Alzheimer's Disease in healthcare settings in the Madison community.

name: UX Club
summary: The UX Club at UW-Madison exists to create a vibrant community for students interested in User Experience (UX) to learn, collaborate, and connect with industry UX professionals.

name: Discussing Research On Psychoactive Substances
summary: Discussing Research On Psychoactive Substances (DROPS) is led by group of graduate students in the Psychoactive Pharmaceutical Investigation program to organize networking opportunities, hold fundraisers, and create scholarship.

name: PulseWatch
summary: We are a free clinic dedicated to raising awareness for hypertension by providing free point-of-care testing to the uninsured/underserved populations in Madison to detect any potential risks of hypertension and/or heart problems.

name: Music Production Club
summary: From beat-making, mixing, sound design, workshops, and more, like-minded students interested in music production get the chance to make connections and collaborate with others to grow as artists.

name: Women's Affordable Housing Network at UW-Madison
summary: Women’s Affordable Housing Network (WAHN) at UW-Madison supports and empowers women seeking to work in the affordable housing sector through partnership, networking, allyship, advocacy, mentorship, and community engagement.

name: Child Care Club
summary: Child Care Club's main goal is to create connection between future professionals with intersection of a goal: well-being of children. Child care club will be volunteering at child care and learning from various professionals.

name: Education Scholars of Social Sciences
summary: Education Scholars of Social Sciences (ESOSS) supports professional development, community-building, and conference preparation for graduate students in the department of Educational Policy Studies, Social Sciences concentration.

name: WaterPower Club
summary: Exposing students across disciplines to the world of hydropower and what it has to offer through events, outreach, professional development, and community engagement, in order to promote a carbon-free future.

name: HUMAN Capital UW
summary: HUMAN Capital supports UW-Madison students in business-related careers by addressing challenges like mental health, imposter syndrome, stress, and inclusivity. It provides resources to help students thrive in professional spheres.

name: Fair Sentencing Project
summary: The Fair Sentencing Project is a student-based organization committed to advancing sentencing reform in Wisconsin through legislative change.

name: Prospera Financial Club
summary: Prospera Financial Club educates students on career development and personal finance, offering tailored sessions and speakers tailored to their goals.

name: Wisconsin Bitcoin Club
summary: Where Wisconsin learns and builds on Bitcoin.

name: Madison Fashion Business Collective
summary: To bridge the gap between fashion, business, and social media, offering members direct access to industry influencers and entrepreneurs. The club serves as a networking hub, a source of education, and a platform to foster connections in the fashion

name: Equilibrium, The Undergraduate Journal of Economics
summary: The Student-Run Undergraduate Economics Journal of UW-Madison

name: Rho Epsilon Iota, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: Rho Epsilon Iota (REI) is a professional real estate fraternity that unites students and alumni to build a national network of real estate professionals. Through hands-on experience, professional development, and community-building.

name: Likelion Wisconsin
summary: LIKELION is a student-run Tech Entrepreneurship Community that helps students solve real-world problems and develop their ideas into competitive products using programming.

name: Queer in STEM
summary: Founded in 2025, we are a student-led organization dedicated to providing educational opportunities, networking and career-building events, and an inclusive community space for queer-identifying and allied students across all STEM disciplines.

name: Nigerian Students Association
summary: +234Badgers is UW–Madison’s Nigerian student association, open to all students interested in Nigerian culture. We build community, promote cultural awareness, and support academic and professional growth through engaging events and initiatives.

name: FinTech@UW
summary: Our mission is to create an enduring FinTech community at UW Madison, while equipping our members with practical experience and expertise to utilize FinTech for their personal and professional growth.

name: Latine Psychology Student Association
summary: This club promotes inclusivity, focusing on Latine students but welcoming all. We aim to broaden our understanding of psychology, prioritize mental health, and support members' well-being through enjoyable, informative events organized by our board.

name: Science-Fiction Club
summary: We will explore the medium of science-fiction and the variety of sub-genres it entails. It is a means to explore the present by imagining the future through media. It is a gathering place for those to share recommendations, explore, and build community.

name: American Library Association - Student Chapter, University of Wisconsin - Madison iSchool.
