summary: The ALA-SC serves as a group to allow Library and Information Science students to make connections with and learn about the American Library Association, the primary organization in the field of Library science.

name: Smart Woman Securities, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: Smart Woman Securities is an organization whose mission is to educate undergraduate women of all majors on investment and finance through educational seminars, guest speaking events, and the chance to propose stock pitches to an investment board.

name: BadgerVC
summary: BadgerVC is a student-run club where students explore venture capital through speaker events, workshops, and projects. If you're interested in startups, investing, or VC, come join us!

name: Badger Bridge
summary: An organization dedicated solely to fostering mentorship between UW-Madison undergraduates and high school students, aiming to bridge the gap between early interest in the pre-health field and the pursuit of a healthcare career.

name: Women Impacting Supply Chain Excellence, UW-Madison
summary: Women Impacting Supply Chain Excellence is an organization that promotes learning in the field of supply chain management and logistics.

name: Women in Orthopedics
summary: This organization fosters a supportive and inclusive community for women interested in orthopedics, offering mentorship and networking opportunities that empower them to thrive in their pursuit of an orthopedic career.

name: Chem-E-Car
summary: The Chem-E-Car Club at UW-Madison researches, builds, and competes in AIChE’s Chem-E-Car competition while providing hands-on engineering experience and fostering an inclusive environment for students.

name: Omani Student Association
summary: OSA represents Omani students in campus events, maintains the Omani identity at UW–Madison, and shares the culture and heritage of Oman with students, faculty, and staff through social and cultural programs.

name: Aviation Club, University of Wisconsin-Madison
summary: The purpose of Aviation Club is to connect students interested in the field of aviation, explore and connect members with career opportunities within aviation, increase student interest in the field, and explore practical applications through projects.

name: Global Perspectives Society
summary: The Global Perspectives Society (GPS) aims to create a dynamic environment where students can engage with diverse cultures, explore global issues, and develop professional networks.

name: Book History & Special Collections Club
summary: The BHSCC is a club about book history, print culture, rare books, and special collections. Our mission is to create an organization that provides opportunities to learn and share ideas about these topics, network, and share professional resources.

name: Bucky Match
summary: Bucky Match is a new founded club. We're building an app that aims to improve the educational experience for all students on campus by integrating all information related to UW-Madison to a useful, intuitive, and accessible software for all students.

name: Mathematics Education Club - Wisconsin Mathematics Council UW Madison Student Affiliate
summary: Mathematics Education Club is a student organization meant to develop an appreciation for the mathematics education profession, provide opportunities for networking within the field of mathematics education, and more.

name: Research to Impact
summary: Research to Impact (R2I) is a UW-Madison student group that helps computer science researchers turn their academic work into real-world applications

name: Scholars of Finance UW-Madison Chapter
summary: Scholars of Finance is a rapidly growing organization on a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow.

name: Cursor at UW-Madison
summary: Cursor at UW-Madison is a student organization dedicated to exploring how AI can transform coding and software development. As official Cursor Campus Ambassadors, we help students discover practical ways to integrate Cursor into their coding workflows.

name: Lakeshore Keepers
summary: Lakeshore Keepers (LSK) is a student-led group focused on keeping the Lakeshore Path clean, safe, and welcoming to all through trash cleanups, micro-pantries, guide signs, and planning future projects aimed at making the paths more accessible.

name: Badger Sprint and Social Club
summary: A club dedicated to providing social interaction through fun running and sprinting workouts designed to be enjoyed at your own pace.

name: Badgers For Life
summary: Badgers For Life promotes the dignity of every human life, educates members to share pro-life views respectfully, and supports pregnant women through local resources, reflecting Christ’s love through compassion and action.

name: Science Relay, UW Madison
summary: Science Relay bridges the gap between STEM Students and Science Leaders/Companies by building student led networking platform that increases access, visibility and offers real-world career insights.

name: AI Hub @ WSB
summary: The student organization paired with the AI Hub faculty division at the Wisconsin School of Business (WSB). Committed to advancing AI at WSB, we promote its ethical use, learn to integrate it across business functions, and educate the community.

name: Undergraduate Business Law Association
summary: The Undergraduate Business Law Association aims to prepare students for careers at the intersection of law, business, and policy by fostering rigorous discussion, real-world case analysis, and professional development.

name: Undergraduate Geology Club
summary: The Undergraduate Geology Club brings together students interested in the geological sciences. Our meetings are both social and academic - providing opportunities for professional development as well as community building.

name: Climate Action Campaign, UW-Madison Chapter
summary: UW-Madison chapter of a non-profit, non-partisan organization aiming to bring awareness to climate change.

name: Sunrise Movement, UW-Madison
summary: Sunrise UW-Madison empowers students to fight climate change and inequality through local action, advocacy, and hope. We push for various campaigns surrounding climate justice, anti-authoritarianism, and a people-powered, sustainable future for all.

name: WiscoHumanoids
summary: WiscoHumanoids aims to bring the emerging field of humanoid robotics to Madison. We believe that humanoid robotics is the future of our workforce, both commercially and domestically. By creating a team on campus we provide a clear path for our students t

name: Madison Industry for Madison students
summary: MIMS is a pipeline for students and campus orgs at UW-Madison with shared music related goals. We aim to expand access to information, collaboration, and career opportunities across the music industries.

name: Environmental Affairs Review of Theory and Law
summary: Environmental Affairs Review of Theory and Law (E.A.R.T.H & L) exists to create a multidisciplinary platform where Madison students interested in writing, art, and journalism, can engage with critical environmental legal theory and policy analysis

name: Students Against Mass Incarceration
summary: Wisconsin incarcerates the highest number of black men per capita. This is an unfortunate fact due to a variety of factors. Accordingly, SAMI seeks to inform students and provide them with opportunities to impact the carceral system in a positive manner.

name: Students for Life of America - UW-Madison
summary: Students for Life members seek to promote respect for life at UW-Madison and on a local, state, and national level, as well as to educate on life issues.

name: Nuclear Engineering and Engineering Physics Graduate Student Association
summary: The purpose of the Nuclear Engineering and Engineering Physics Graduate Student Association is to provide professional and social support for UW-Madison nuclear engineering graduate students.

name: Indigenous Graduate Students University of Wisconsin
summary: A community for Indigenous Graduate Students and Allies at UW-Madison.

name: One for the World UW-Madison
summary: One for the World UW-Madison is the Madison chapter of One for the World -- a global initiative with the mission of encouraging students and young professionals to pledge to give 1% of their income to effective charities.

name: Banned Books Club
summary: A student-led organization that reads and discusses challenged and banned books to understand their social impact. The club promotes intellectual freedom, critical thinking, and respectful dialogue around diverse and often marginalized voices.

name: Students for a National Health Program, UW-Madison
summary: SNaHP works to educate health professional students about the benefits of a single-payer healthcare system. We work toward a single-payer national health program in Madison, Wisconsin, and beyond.

name: Wisconsin SmallSat
summary: WiS³ aims to develop technically proficient engineers by designing, integrating, and launching reliable satellite and other space systems for competitive missions, while fostering teamwork, leadership, and engineering rigor.

name: National Defense Society | UW-Madison Chapter
summary: NDS provides a platform for students from all academic disciplines to come together driven by a shared passion for national defense and security through university chapters, technical projects, and network engagements.

name: Korean Badgers Investment Group
summary: K-BIG (Korean Badger Investment Group) is a premier student-led investment organization at the University of Wisconsin-Madison. Our mission is to bridge the gap between academic economic theory and practical financial application.

name: If You're Reading This Wisconsin
summary: If You’re Reading This is a national organization active at 30+ schools. IYRT Wisconsin centers UW–Madison students, sharing real stories of mental health struggles to reduce stigma, build connection, and provide a supportive outlet for those navigating.

name: Microbiology Engagement Group
summary: The Microbiology Engagement Group is a group of passionate microbiologists from across UW-Madison who share a passion for science engagement and communication.
